sources: GitHub READMEs read directly from raw.githubusercontent.com: UniGuide/Chatalogue, cellmapvnn, rainwater-monitoring-system, ProsodyAnalysis, multimodel-learning-disability-detection, space-pirates, PocketBand, Chronicle, DARTS
LinkedIn announcement post for PocketBand (Music Hackspace x Berklee, MuseHub track)
_meta.coverage_note: GitHub API rate limit on the shared IP remained exhausted across retries, and the repositories tab blocks scraping, so all 24 repos could not be enumerated programmatically. All 9 substantive project repos were read in full from raw.githubusercontent.com. Additional name-probing found only 'preetham-portfolio' (the portfolio source for preethamprathipati.com; README empty). Zenith has no README on its default branch. Remaining repos are minor/forks/course artifacts and are intentionally not listed as projects.
_meta.primary_directive: Project entries are grounded in actual repo contents. Where the resume/LinkedIn claim something the public repo does not show, it is recorded in `discrepancies_to_reconcile` rather than silently kept or deleted.
_meta.portfolio_repo: github.com/artisticdrake/preetham-portfolio (source for preethamprathipati.com; README empty - add one)

header.name: Preetham Prathipati
header.headline_linkedin: Machine Learning Engineer | GenAI, RAG & LLMs | NLP & Computer Vision | MS CS @ Boston University '26 | 2x Hackathon Winner
header.work_authorization: F-1 OPT / STEM OPT (work-authorized ~3 years, no near-term sponsorship needed; in-US change-of-status to H-1B is exempt from the $100k surcharge)

summaries.text: ML Engineer with an MS in Computer Science (Boston University, May 2026), focused on taking applied ML and Generative AI from prototype to production. Won $4,000 at Bio-IT World Hackathon 2026 for NeST-VNN, an interpretable cancer-outcome model in PyTorch with a Bedrock RAG layer. Builds end-to-end systems with rigorous evaluation, documented code, and a bias toward shipping measurable value.

Applied ML / GenAI engineer focused on RAG, LLM applications, and NLP pipelines. Built a 9-stage local-first NLP course assistant (custom spaCy NER + ML intent classification + deterministic SQL + RAG) and a Bedrock-backed RAG interpretation layer for an award-winning genomics model.

ML engineer focused on interpretable AI for healthcare and genomics. Built NeST-VNN (interpretable Visible Neural Network for cancer outcomes, PyTorch + AWS Bedrock) winning $4,000 at Bio-IT World 2026, plus Chronicle, a CHF remote-monitoring pipeline (Oura Ring + ElevenLabs voice agent + Claude clinical synthesis).

Full-stack ML engineer who ships complete products, including real-time multi-device systems. Built two phone-as-controller systems over WebSocket/Socket.IO (a music jam station and a multiplayer game) plus a full-stack clinical dashboard and an LLM-powered job-application tracker.

ML engineer with computer-vision and edge-deployment experience: a campus-wide CV/OCR rainwater monitoring system on Raspberry Pi, YOLO/OpenCV vehicle detection for adaptive traffic control, and a real-time sound-to-tactile wearable, alongside deep NLP and GenAI work.

experiences.note: LinkedIn shows this ended May 2026; the resume lists it as 'Present'. Reconcile.

bullets.text: Built a Python data-automation tool (pandas/openpyxl) that ingests, validates, and transforms monthly package-report data into scannable barcode records; packaged as a standalone Windows executable and shipped to production, cutting processing time ~26% at peak and still in active use 15+ months later.
Analyzed annual operations data to identify bottlenecks and turned findings into the insights management used in monthly reporting and decisions.
Built a standardized, color-coded residence-to-location reference adopted as the routing standard across every campus mailroom.

Deployed a CV + OCR rainwater-monitoring system on Raspberry Pi 3B+ with IP cameras (OpenCV edge/line detection + Tesseract OCR) and automated email alerts; mass-produced and deployed campus-wide, improving flood-response time ~30%.
Built a YOLO + OpenCV vehicle-detection and traffic-analysis model enabling congestion-based automated signal control and long-term trend analysis.
Built and evaluated ML/DL models (scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch) across CV and IoT problems on Linux; contributed to a speech-API live-translation tool and a gaze-detection exam-integrity system.

experiences.org: International Institute of Information Technology Hyderabad (IIITH)

bullets.text: Designed a novel prosody dataset for speech-to-speech translation by applying Spleeter voice/music source separation plus energy profiling (Librosa/NumPy) to movie audio — a cross-domain contribution from music-production expertise.

Integrated the dataset into the lab's translation pipeline (Git-versioned), improving translation accuracy 8–10% with measured gains in naturalness and intelligibility; presented to a cohort of 20+ researchers.

projects.name: NeST-VNN / CellMap-VNN — Interpretable Cancer Outcome Prediction
projects.summary: Interpretable Visible Neural Network whose architecture mirrors a biological ontology hierarchy (NeST / Cell Map) so predictions are explainable at the level of biological systems, not individual genes. Full pipeline: cBioPortal data acquisition, ontology-structured training, per-patient explainability, LLM clinical interpretation, and an interactive web app.

bullets.text: Built an interpretable Visible Neural Network in PyTorch whose nodes mirror a hierarchical biological ontology, yielding pathway-level explanations via RLIPP scores, gene scores, and boolean-logic gates rather than black-box outputs.

Engineered the end-to-end pipeline: cBioPortal ingestion (mutations/CNV/fusions) -> transform -> PyTorch training (AdamW, early stopping, gradient masking for ontology sparsity) with MLflow tracking across 20 Optuna trials -> hierarchy annotation.
Built an LLM interpretation pipeline (AWS Bedrock Claude, OpenAI fallback) that turns per-patient model outputs into plain-English clinical reasoning, stored in a structured SQLite DB.

Built the NeST-STRING Explorer: a unified Flask app (port 5001) with a population dashboard, per-patient LLM-interpretation cards, and an interactive Cytoscape.js hierarchy map wired to STRING PPI networks and live NDEx/STRING/MyGene proxies.
Won the $4,000 prize at Bio-IT World Hackathon 2026 (Fair Data Track); judges cited excellence in interpretability. Builds on Nature Cancer (2024) and Cancer Discovery (2024) methodology.

projects.name: UniGuide / Chatalogue — University Course NLP Assistant
projects.summary: A local-first, ML-powered 9-stage modular NLP pipeline that answers student questions about courses, instructors, schedules, and locations. Runs entirely offline except for optional LLM enhancement.

bullets.text: Architected a 9-stage NLP pipeline: ML intent classification (LogReg + SentenceTransformers) -> custom spaCy NER (COURSE_NAME, COURSE_CODE, INSTRUCTOR, BUILDING, TIME, WEEKDAY, SECTION) -> conversation-context resolution -> fuzzy course mapping -> safe parameterized SQL generation -> SQLite execution -> context update -> RAG prompt construction -> optional GPT-4.1-mini response.
Handles compound, multi-course, multi-attribute queries and contextual follow-ups ('Who teaches it?', 'Where does that class meet?') via an explicit ConversationContext store.
Deterministic SQL layer makes database access safe and structured; LLM is fully optional (system works in SQL-only mode without an API key).

projects.discrepancy_note: Public repo shows a local-first Tkinter app on SQLite with spaCy NER + LogReg intent + optional GPT-4.1-mini. The resume/v1 profile instead claim BERT fine-tuning (98% NER), FastAPI microservices, PostgreSQL vector storage, Docker + Jenkins CI/CD, Redis (-35% latency), React/Vite, and 30+ deployed users. None of that infrastructure appears in the public repo. See discrepancies_to_reconcile.

projects.name: PocketBand — Phone-as-Instrument AI Jam Station (NL-controlled synth)
tech_stack: Web Audio API (from-scratch polyphonic subtractive synth)
Stable Audio 3 (drums; FastAPI service in WSL2/CUDA, RTX 4080)
projects.summary: A phone-as-instrument jam station: scan a QR and your phone becomes a live controller for a synthesizer running on your PC. Scale-locked chord wheel, beatbox looper, and gyro-driven effects. The standout feature judges responded to most: you design sounds by typing plain English ('lush pad', 'dark analog bass') and an LLM maps the description to 20 synthesis parameters, reconfiguring the synth live.

bullets.text: Designed the standout feature: a natural-language-to-synthesis-parameters mapping where Claude turns a plain-English description ('lush pad', 'dark analog bass') into 20 live synth parameters, reconfiguring the synth in real time.
Built a polyphonic subtractive synthesizer from scratch in the Web Audio API (oscillators, filters, ADSR envelopes, effects), with presets reverse-engineered from real Serum patches.
Solved the browser-audio timing problem that breaks most web jam tools: a bar-quantized audio clock syncing phone and desktop over WebSocket so loops and layers stay in time.

Integrated three generative-AI services for what each does best: Suno (vocal stems), Stable Audio 3 (drum generation), Claude (NL->parameters) — with graceful fallbacks for every AI call so the live demo never breaks.
Owned the backend and real-time architecture (phone<->desktop relay, AI proxies, audio clock) in a 36-hour two-person build with Athul Thulasidasan on the interface.
Placed 3rd in the MuseHub track at the Music Hackspace x Berklee Music Technology Hackathon, judged by researchers from Google DeepMind, Suno, and Ableton.

projects.name: Chronicle — AI Remote Monitoring for CHF Patients
projects.summary: Detects early cardiac decompensation in congestive-heart-failure patients by combining passive Oura Ring biometrics with an AI-conducted voice check-in, then routes an AI-generated clinical brief to the care team before an ER visit.

bullets.text: Built the pipeline: Oura anomaly -> ElevenLabs AI outbound call conducting an 8-question conversational SOAP assessment -> Claude synthesizes transcript + biometrics into a clinical brief -> clinician approve/escalate/dismiss -> patient SMS.
Designed a 0–175-point decompensation scoring engine (dyspnea, orthopnea, edema, weight gain, missed diuretics, multi-signal Oura anomaly) with urgent/review/monitor thresholds.
Added adaptive alert suppression: after 3 consecutive clinician rejections for a patient, the review threshold raises automatically to prevent alert fatigue.
Built a React/Vite/Recharts clinician dashboard (plus patient and dev views). Selected by FOMO for investor introductions and Foundry follow-up.

projects.name: Space Pirates (Rogue Space Pirates) — Phone-Controlled Multiplayer Game
projects.summary: A local multiplayer party brawler where each player's smartphone is a motion controller. Game runs on a TV/monitor; up to 5 phones connect as gyroscope controllers. Themed around teaching real cybersecurity/data-privacy concepts through gameplay.

bullets.text: Three real-time components: a Godot 4 game (GDScript) <-WebSocket-> a Node.js relay <-Socket.IO/HTTPS-> phone browser controllers, forwarding gyro input at ~60fps via volatile emit to keep controls live.
Implemented server-side slot/color management with state replay so a reconnecting Godot client instantly resyncs the lobby; six ship classes, five game modes, and per-viewport split-screen.
Solved the iOS secure-context constraint (deviceorientation only fires over HTTPS) with auto-generated self-signed certs; generated all SFX procedurally at runtime as raw 16-bit PCM.
Built largely solo at SharkHack 2026 after a teammate dropped out; judges played it enthusiastically.

projects.discrepancy_note: Resume/v1 profile list the stack as 'Three.js, WebSockets, Node.js, JavaScript'. The actual repo is a Godot 4 / GDScript game with a Node.js + Socket.IO bridge — there is no Three.js. Correct this everywhere.

projects.name: Zenith — AI-Powered Job Application Tracker (SaaS)
projects.summary: Full-stack LLM-powered tracker with a deterministic resume–JD matching engine and a resume builder.

bullets.text: Built a full-stack tracker (React/Vite + Node/Express + Supabase + Google OAuth) with a resume builder (Jake's Resume live preview, LaTeX export, one-page auto-fit).

Engineered a deterministic resume–JD matching engine (canonical skill dictionary, regex section parsing, implied-skill inference) to replace unreliable LLM scoring, plus a GPT-4o-mini autofill endpoint extracting structured job data from raw URLs and SHA-256 resume dedup.

projects.action_item: No README found on the default branch. Add one (architecture, the deterministic matcher rationale, screenshots) before listing it prominently.

projects.name: Predicting Learning Difficulties in Children (ML Classification)
projects.summary: Multiple classification models predicting child learning difficulty on a NHIS dataset (7,439 records, 353 variables, ~9:1 imbalance). Best model: Random Forest with under-sampling.

bullets.text: Best model (Random Forest + under-sampling) reached 86.5% balanced accuracy, 86.1% sensitivity (TPR-Yes), 86.8% specificity, AUC 0.938 — at a fixed 0.5 threshold (no tuning allowed).

Engineered missing-indicator features (>5% missingness), median/mode imputation, near-zero-variance filtering tuned to preserve rare predictive medical conditions, and removed a data-leaking variable (ldnw_c) after review.

projects.discrepancy_note: README states the dataset is the 2024 NHIS child dataset; the v1 profile/resume said 2019. Confirm the correct year. (Class split per README: 89.8% No / 10.2% Yes.)

projects.name: Real-Time Rainwater Monitoring System
projects.summary: Computer-vision + OCR water-level monitoring with automated email alerts, day/night capable, power-efficient 10-minute cycles. Deployed campus-wide; cut flood-response time ~30%.

bullets.text: Captured water-tub images via IP camera, detected levels with OpenCV edge/line detection, extracted numeric readings via Tesseract OCR, and sent automated threshold-based email alerts.

projects.name: ProsodyAnalysis — Voice/Music Separation for Speech-to-Speech Translation
projects.summary: Novel technique isolating voice and music from movie audio (Spleeter + energy profiling) to auto-generate a prosody dataset for speech-to-speech translation. IIIT Hyderabad, SRISHTI'23.

bullets.text: Built an energy-profiling framework distinguishing 'only voice' vs 'voice+music' segments and a comparative-visualization pipeline; produced a cleaned, labeled dataset for prosody extraction and TTS/translation conditioning.
Reported gains: prosody-feature-extraction accuracy 78.2%->86.5% (+8.3pp), model intelligibility 84.0%->91.2% (+7.2pp), subjective naturalness 6.9->7.8/10.

projects.name: DARTS — Deaf Assistance Real-Time Sound-to-Tactile Wearable
projects.summary: Wearable that translates environmental sounds into tactile feedback for the hearing-impaired, running fully on a Raspberry Pi 4: continuous dual-mic listening, sound classification, direction/proximity estimation, and intensity-scaled vibration alerts.

bullets.text: Real-time pipeline: dual-mic capture -> MFCC/spectrogram features -> neural-network sound classifier -> TDOA + amplitude-ratio proximity/direction estimation -> PWM-driven vibration patterns (e.g. siren = continuous buzz, speech = rhythmic pulses).
Achieved ~95% classification accuracy across 20+ critical sound classes and <200ms end-to-end latency on-device; <150g wearable prototype, auto-starting via systemd.

awards.title: Bio-IT World Hackathon 2026 — Prize Winner ($4,000), Fair Data Track
awards.detail: Team of 4; judges cited excellence in model interpretability (NeST-VNN).

awards.title: Music Hackspace x Berklee Music Technology Hackathon — 3rd Place (MuseHub track)
awards.detail: For PocketBand (phone-as-instrument AI jam station). Judged by researchers from Google DeepMind, Suno, and Ableton. Built in 36 hours with Athul Thulasidasan.

awards.title: National ML Ranking — 2nd of 364 (Foundations of Modern Machine Learning, IIIT Hyderabad)

certifications.title: 8th Summer School on AI — Research Intern (Machine Learning)
Summer Research Internship on Technological Innovations — SRISHTI'23

education.degree: Bachelor of Technology, Computer Science & Engineering

skills.note: GDScript and audio-ML (Web Audio, Spleeter, SA3, Suno) are repo-proven but missing from the current resume's skills section — consider adding for music/AI and real-time roles.

positioning_assets.flagship: NeST-VNN ($4,000 Bio-IT World win, interpretable cancer ML)
positioning_assets.production_proof: 9-stage NLP course assistant (deterministic SQL + RAG); CV/OCR system deployed campus-wide
positioning_assets.shipping_pattern: 3 hackathon results in one quarter: Bio-IT 2nd/$4K, Music Hackspace 3rd, FOMO investor pick (Chronicle)
positioning_assets.real_time_systems_proof: Two independent phone-as-controller systems over Socket.IO/WebSocket (PocketBand, Space Pirates) sharing a self-signed-cert + low-latency pattern
positioning_assets.differentiator: ML depth + full-stack + audio/music-AI creative angle; Boston biotech/health-AI fit (NeST-VNN, Chronicle)
positioning_assets.target_roles: ML Engineer / Applied Science / GenAI Engineer (per latest LinkedIn post); also AI Solutions / Forward Deployed Engineer
positioning_assets.creative_ai_proof: PocketBand: from-scratch Web Audio synth + LLM natural-language sound design, judged by DeepMind/Suno/Ableton — a rare, memorable signal for AI x creative-tools roles.

discrepancies_to_reconcile.action: Fix the resume; verify the dead one isn't linked anywhere.
Pick one consistent contact email across resume/LinkedIn/GitHub. The bu.edu address may expire after graduation.

discrepancies_to_reconcile.resume: BERT fine-tuning (98% NER), FastAPI microservices, PostgreSQL vector storage, Docker + Jenkins CI/CD, Redis (-35% latency), React/Vite, 30+ users
discrepancies_to_reconcile.repo_truth: Local-first Tkinter app on SQLite; spaCy NER + LogReg/SentenceTransformers intent; optional GPT-4.1-mini
discrepancies_to_reconcile.action: Major mismatch. Either the public repo is an older/local version (then push the deployed code or stop claiming infra you can't show) or correct the resume to match the repo. An interviewer who clicks will notice.

discrepancies_to_reconcile.repo_truth: Godot 4 / GDScript game + Node.js + Socket.IO bridge (no Three.js)
discrepancies_to_reconcile.action: Correct everywhere; GitHub already tags the repo as GDScript.
Reconcile; if it ended, say so.
Add one before featuring the repo.
